english: killed his mate by a stroke of his bill Bhar.; *tsá-ge-la gyob* (*tsa ge la rgyob*) W. throw into the centre! hit the mark! sdóṅ-po-la brgyab póg (sdong po la brgyab phog) (the ray of light) fell upon the stem Glr.; dé-la ču rgyob (de la chu rgyob) sprinkle this with water! Pth.; pyugs ṅágs-seb-tu rgyáb-pa (phyugs nags seb tu rgyab pa) to let the cattle run into the wood; *źag-dáṅ gyab-če* (*zhag dang rgyab ce*) W. to put down the date, to date; *la gyab-če* (*la rgyab ce*) to cross a mountain pass. — rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa) is particularly used in W. in many phrases, whilst in C. rgyág-pa (rgyag pa), and in B. 'débs-pa ('debs pa) are more in use, as may be seen by referring to the several substantives, e.g. *lud gyáb-če* (*lud rgyab ce*) W. to throw dung upon the fields, to manure; *par gyáb-pa* (*par rgyab pa*) C., and *gyáb-če* (*rgyab ce*) W., to print; gyáb-pa (rgyab pa) stands also alone, elliptically: *ka gyab dug* (*ka rgyab dug*) here (is printed) the letter ka.
Med., Cs.: 'a kind of salt, like crystal'.

1. victory, in certain phrases: *gyal tób-če* (*rgyal thob ce*) W. to gain the victory, to conquer, overcome; to win a law-suit, opp. to *pám póg-če* (*pham phog ce*). — 2. Schr. and Sch.: rgyal-rgyán (rgyal rgyan) a pledge, a stake, rgyal btsúg-pa (rgyal btsug pa) to bet, wager, gamble Sch.; rgyal bźag-pa (rgyal bzhag pa) to contend with an other person about the first place, to try to get the precedence (?) Sch.; perh. also ornament, v. sub rgód-pa (rgod pa) 2. — 3. fine, penalty, for theft C. — 4. n. of two lunar mansions, v. sub rgyu-skár (rgyu skar); rgyál-gyi zlá-ba (rgyal gyi zla ba), skár-ma rgyál-la báb-pai nyín-par (skar ma rgyal la bab pai nyin par), skár-ma rgyal daṅ dzom-par (skar ma rgyal dang dzom par), dpyíd-zla rá-bai skár-ma rgyál-gyi nyín-par (dpyid zla ra bai skar ma rgyal gyi nyin par), are dates relating to them. — 5. for rgyál-po (rgyal po) and rgyál-ba (rgyal ba).

I. vb. neut. to be victorious, to obtain the victory, always with the sbst. in the nom. (not instr.) case, and gen. with las (las), over or against, nág-poi pyógs-las (nag poi phyogs las) over the powers of darkness, 'jigs-pa-las ('jigs pa las) over fright, fear; also gyúl-lás (g-yul las) in battle; prob. also tsod ltá-ba-las (tshod lta ba las) to pass an examination successfully; but also without las (las), rtsód-pa (rtsod pa) (to win) in a contest Glr.: very frq. rgyál-bar gyúr-ba (rgyal bar gyur ba) id.; ṅa pám soṅ kyod gyal soṅ (nga pham song khyod rgyal song) I have lost, thou hast won (also in business): to be acquitted, to gain a law-suit; dmag-'krúgs-kyi rgyal-pám-gyi rnas-tsúl kól (dmag 'khrugs kyi rgyal pham gyi gnas tshul bkol) send (us) news concerning the progress of the war; in a similar sense: rgyal pam ci-ltar byuṅ (rgyal pham ci ltar byung) B.; rgyál-bar gyúr-čig (rgyal bar gyur cig), rgyál-ba daṅ tse-riṅ-bar śog čig (rgyal ba dang tshe ring bar shog cig) victory and long life! Dzl. II. sbst. 1. (Ssk. जय) the act of conquering, the victory, *di gyál-wa tob* (*'di rgyal ba thob*) C. this bears away the palm or prize, this is the most excellent of all. — 2. the conquering party or person, he that prevails, the conqueror (opp to pám-pa (pham pa) the conquered, vanquished). Much more frq.: 3. the most high, Buddha (Ssk. जिन), rgyál-bai sku (rgyal bai sku) his person, rgyál-bai bka (rgyal bai bka) his word; rgyal daṅ de sras (rgyal dang de sras) (po. instead of rgyál-ba daṅ dei sras (rgyal ba dang dei sras)) Buddha and his children, his disciples Pth. 1, 1; rgyál-ba góṅ-ma (rgyal ba gong ma) the highest Buddha, God, Mil. — 4. rgyál-ba rin-po-čé (rgyal ba rin po che) His Highness, His supreme Majesty, title of the Dalai Lama. III. adj. 1. conquering, superior, eminent, excellent, rnám-par rgyál-bai kaṅ-bzáṅs (rnam par rgyal bai khang bzangs) the most magnificent palace (of Indra) Glr. — 2. W. (gen. pronounced *gyálla* (*rgyal la*), in Pur. *rgyál-wa* (*rgyal ba*)) good, instead of bzáṅ-po (bzang po); *gyál-la dád-če* (*rgyal la dad ce*) or *lús-če* (*lus ce*) to continue in good condition, entire, uninjured; mā gyalla (ma rgyal la) excellent! capital! Comp. and deriv., belonging partly to rgyál-ba (rgyal ba), partly to rgyál-po (rgyal po): *gyal-kár* (*rgyal dkar*) window C. — *gyál-ka* (*rgyal kha*), *ka-gyál* (*kha rgyal*) victory, gain, advantage W. — rgyal-kág (rgyal khag) country, empire. — rgyal-káms (rgyal khams) 1. kingdom. 2. realm, dominion of Buddha, the world. — rgyal-krid (rgyal khrid) Ld. for rgyal-srid (rgyal srid). — rgyal-khríms (rgyal khrims) v. khrims (khrims). — rgyal-góṅ (rgyal gong) n. of a demon Mil. — rgyal-brgyúd (rgyal brgyud), and rgyal-rábs (rgyal rabs) 1. succession of kings of the same line or family, dynasty Glr. 2. a single generation of a dynasty, rgyal-brgyúd lṅá-bcu-na (rgyal brgyud lnga bcu na) in the fiftieth degree (in the line of descent). — rgyál-sgo (rgyal sgo) principal door, entrance-door, gate C. — rgyal-sgrúṅs (rgyal sgrungs), legend of the kings, esp. that of Gesar. — rgyal-čén bźi (rgyal chen bzhi) the four kings of the spirits or guardians of the universe
